207304.explanation: Woerden & Democracy believes residents deserve more direct influence on major decisions. A referendum can help with directional decisions, provided it is used carefully and the municipal council remains ultimately responsible.

207306.explanation: Residents often know best what their neighborhood needs. Additional neighborhood budgets give room to initiatives and strengthen cohesion and livability.

207307.explanation: Citizen assemblies can provide new insights and more support. They are advisory and intended as a complement to municipal council decision-making. Unlike lotteries, other methods are also acceptable.

207310.explanation: Woerden & Democracy wants to focus on jobs for skilled tradespeople. This strengthens the local economy and offers opportunities for practically trained residents.

207312.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve trust and space. However, agreements about nuisance and livability for neighbors must be respected.

207314.explanation: High fees can slow down construction and sustainability measures. Lower costs can accelerate this and make investments more attractive. However, it must remain cost-covering.

207315.explanation: More generous terrace hours increase the vibrancy of the city center, provided nuisance to neighbors is prevented.

207320.explanation: Residents with low incomes often live in social rental housing. It is the responsibility of the homeowner, especially the housing corporation, to properly insulate these homes. The municipality should not take over tasks that belong to landlords to avoid overlap and confusion.

207321.explanation: Mowing less promotes biodiversity. Safety and a well-maintained appearance must be maintained. Mowing less to save costs is not the intention. After a complaint about nuisance, the municipality must mow actively.

207322.explanation: Proper maintenance of streets, greenery and playgrounds is essential for safety and livability. This is one of the core tasks of a municipality. Woerden has a huge backlog to catch up on here.

207323.explanation: Pay-as-you-throw can encourage waste separation and reduce residual waste. However, this must not lead to littering or illegal dumping. The municipality must provide good facilities, fair rates and enforcement to prevent misuse.

207324.explanation: A levy per resident is fairer for singles and small households, who currently pay relatively much. At the same time, larger households must not be disproportionately burdened. Implementation must remain simple and affordable for everyone.

207326.explanation: Solidarity is important, but the major housing shortage requires careful and balanced distribution.

207327.explanation: Investing in integration through language, work and participation prevents problems in the long term.

207329.explanation: Good language skills are essential to participate in work, education and society. They also contribute to a safe Woerden because people adopt Dutch norms and values faster.

207331.explanation: Swimming skills are important for safety, especially in a water-rich municipality like Woerden.

207332.explanation: Culture is important, but new construction must be financially feasible and broadly supported. Financially, we as a municipality cannot afford to build a new theater in the coming four years.

207334.explanation: Maintaining two swimming pools is a large and structural expense for residents. Choices must be made, but not without involving residents. Commercial exploitation, with clear conditions for affordability and accessibility, is an option for Woerden & Democracy.

207335.explanation: Visible enforcement increases the feeling of safety and helps tackle nuisance more quickly and effectively. With only a few officers for the whole municipality, capacity is insufficient. Woerden & Democracy wants enough public order officers, visible in neighborhoods and villages, with attention to prevention and enforcement.

207336.explanation: Camera surveillance is not an end in itself. At demonstrably unsafe locations, the municipality must quickly and purposefully deploy cameras. This requires clear choices, good evaluation, and respect for privacy, not large-scale or standard application.

207337.explanation: Woerden must install more streetlights in the rural area. Safety is important, but lighting must fit the landscape and nature.

207338.explanation: Facilitating a starter loan is not a municipal task and is financially unfeasible.

207343.explanation: First realize the agreed plans. Expansion only with support and necessity.

207344.explanation: Splitting homes can help with the housing shortage and better use of existing houses. The municipality should facilitate this through regulations.

207345.explanation: Further expansion of wind turbines in Woerden is excluded. The implementation of existing plans must also be carefully reconsidered to exclude health risks for residents and ensure support.

207346.explanation: Supporting more people in time prevents bigger social and financial problems. The poverty threshold should be increased to encourage independent earning capacity.

207347.explanation: Participation is important and increases chances of an independent life without reliance on social assistance. Work should be meaningful and, where possible, suitable. Sitting at home is not an option.

207348.explanation: Targeted subsidized jobs help people towards regular employment. Results must be closely monitored and subsidies must never be structural.

207349.explanation: Debt relief remains necessary for low-income people to make ends meet. At the same time, the municipality should reduce this annually.

207350.explanation: Work must pay off. Temporary supplements prevent people from financially losing out when transitioning to work. This support should focus on growth and independence so that work remains attractive and people do not fall back on social assistance.

207354.explanation: 30 km/h increases road safety in places where many people gather, such as residential areas and around schools. Woerden & Democracy does not find a general 30 km/h limit for all of Woerden appropriate. Only where necessary and with visible measures does 30 km/h have effect.

207355.explanation: Parking on the edge of a neighborhood can contribute to livability and free up space on streets. This must always be practical, safe, and easily accessible. Woerden & Democracy prefers customized solutions: looking at each neighborhood and situation to find what works best for residents.

207356.explanation: The usefulness and necessity of a western bypass must first be convincingly demonstrated. Additionally, there must be support among residents and a substantial contribution from the province. Without these conditions, realization is not responsible for Woerden.

207360.explanation: Free parking can encourage visits to the city center. However, two hours of free parking puts too much financial pressure on the municipal budget. Woerden & Democracy supports free parking, but only in an affordable and responsible form.

207362.explanation: Heat networks are not a task for the municipality. Municipal participation means all residents pay, while the heat network is often mandatory for a limited group. This limits freedom of choice and often leads to higher costs for users. Sustainability requires freedom of choice and ma

207363.explanation: Prevention and awareness contribute to a healthier society and lower healthcare costs.

207304.explanation: Hold a referendum on major issues simultaneously with elections to keep costs low. Inwonersbelangen has advocated this for years, but other parties recently blocked establishing a referendum.

207306.explanation: Support neighborhood residents who are involved and want to take on tasks with more funds so they can carry them out well.

207307.explanation: It is too simplistic to have one group give advice. Inwonersbelangen sees more neighborhood-, district-, or core-focused solutions. For major issues, a referendum can be used.

207310.explanation: The municipality should invest money and ensure sufficient practically skilled workers are trained by TechnoHUB and Futura College. This helps existing and new companies.

207312.explanation: It is good that opening hours are now determined in consultation between the municipality, hospitality sector, and restaurants, also considering residents.

207314.explanation: Building permits must not become a cash cow but should be realistic regarding the costs incurred.

207315.explanation: It is good that there is always consultation about what fits to open terraces. For large events, it could be somewhat more flexible, but taking into account residents in the city and villages. We must be careful this does not become a year-round situation.

207320.explanation: Insulating houses immediately saves on energy bills. It is good that people who really cannot afford it can turn to the municipality. Landlords also have a role here.

207321.explanation: Mowing less often often creates a flower-rich grass field. But in many places, mowing is needed to ensure safety at corners and intersections.

207322.explanation: There have been years of cuts to public spaces. This cannot continue because Woerden would fall below the minimum. Therefore, sharper choices will have to be made.

207323.explanation: We are completely against introducing a pay-per-kilo waste disposal system (diftar).

207324.explanation: The current system is fine. Do not change it or else waste tourism will occur and residents will dump waste elsewhere.

207326.explanation: Inwonersbelangen believes these refugees should be placed on the municipality's housing waiting list just like all other housing seekers.

207327.explanation: The national government provides funds for the integration of newcomers. No additional money from the municipal budget is needed for this.

207329.explanation: The municipality should no longer provide language lessons to newcomers. Volunteers who spend time helping people learn the language can be supported.

207331.explanation: In our water-rich Woerden, it is important that all children can receive swimming lessons. If this can be arranged through schools as it was done before, that would be great.

207332.explanation: Inwonersbelangen is proud of our pocket theater, the Klooster. We hear and see the problems and costs of a new theater and find this step undesirable and unaffordable for Woerden.

207334.explanation: Who wouldn’t want a new swimming pool? But closing H2O goes too far for us. It is a basic facility in Harmelen. Batenstein can be renovated as we have proposed for the town hall, which might be affordable. New construction now costs 35 million.

207335.explanation: Inwonersbelangen chooses a BOA (special enforcement officer) or an accessible neighborhood police officer for each neighborhood and village to improve safety and visibility.

207336.explanation: Sometimes it is necessary to place a camera permanently or temporarily to better control disturbance and safety. Examples are Tournoysveld and the station area.

207337.explanation: Inwonersbelangen believes residents must feel safe due to streetlight lighting, including under bridges and viaducts.

207338.explanation: A starter loan only applies to a few lucky people. Inwonersbelangen focuses on building more starter homes.

207343.explanation: If more than the 90 homes can be realized in a green environment with good accessibility and there is support for this, we are open to more.

207344.explanation: It should be made easier above all to realize this.

207345.explanation: We think the three turbines in the Reijerscop are already too many. Now there are plans to place turbines south of the A12 near Cattenbroek and Papenkop. We are adamantly against this for the health of humans and animals.

207346.explanation: The municipality does not engage in income politics. That is the responsibility of the national government.

207347.explanation: It would be beneficial for a social assistance recipient to gain experience and participate in society.

207348.explanation: It would help to get unemployed people back to work as quickly as possible.

207349.explanation: We do not consider full debt forgiveness an option. For residents with low incomes, some municipal taxes can be temporarily waived for certain components.

207350.explanation: As Residents' Interests, we see this as a good support until a new job is found.

207354.explanation: Residents' Interests chooses 30 km/h zones where the street is or can be designed accordingly and where enforcement is possible.

207355.explanation: Increasingly, it is necessary to have flanking parking policies on the outskirts because car mobility is still increasing and we live in a commuter city.

207356.explanation: This has been intended for 40 years but no party has fulfilled its promises. Not even with the Rembrandt Bridge and the 5 million wasted there. Ring roads west as well as east are needed to get Woerden out of traffic chaos.

207360.explanation: To be a welcoming city - also for the surrounding areas - the first two hours of parking in garages should be free. It would be great if shopkeepers also contribute to this, as it is in their interest too.

207362.explanation: The municipality must stimulate and facilitate the installation of heat networks. But first, to do this realistically, sufficient support from residents in the neighborhood is needed.

207363.explanation: All information, including practical health advice, helps all residents live healthier, which also reduces costs and keeps our healthcare affordable.

207304.explanation: Important decisions can have a big impact on the environment of our residents. Therefore, it is wise that all residents of our municipality can make their voices heard.

207306.explanation: Residents often have good ideas but often lack the funds to implement them. In consultation with the municipality, good ideas can be evaluated and executed. Currently, little money is made available for neighborhood improvements by residents.

207307.explanation: We highly value the opinions of residents. However, we believe that citizens' assemblies on important topics should be conducted through an advisory referendum. This way, all residents are heard.

207310.explanation: The municipality has no role in the business operations of entrepreneurs. For example, the great example of the TechnoHUB also originated from the initiative of entrepreneurs. The municipality has cooperated here, and it is important we continue to do so in the future.

207312.explanation: Good agreements have been made with owners of cafes and restaurants, considering both the owners themselves and the neighbors.

207314.explanation: The problem with permitting, for example for construction, is not the money but the long procedures and a web of too many rules. This needs improvement.

207315.explanation: We do not experience the extended opening hours of terraces as a problem in Woerden or surrounding villages. Here too, we believe there is a good balance between the livability of residents and the entrepreneur’s terrace opening hours.

207320.explanation: Residents with limited financial means find it hardest to insulate their homes. They need to be continuously supported by the municipality.

207321.explanation: LijstvanderDoes has been committed for years to flourishing verges and more biodiversity. Mowing verges and lawns less frequently benefits this because without the bee, we perish! Traffic safety must still be taken into account.

207322.explanation: Public spaces have long been managed at a minimal level, leaving its mark. Unsafe situations due to low maintenance are increasingly occurring if we do not intervene now. Otherwise, more bridges will be closed or more street lamps will fail.

207323.explanation: Currently, we use a positive incentive. Residents get money back by putting out residual waste less often. We want to keep it that way. Residents should not be punished because they have to dispose of their waste.

207324.explanation: It depends on the implementation. We are in favor if it becomes cheaper for single-person households. But we do not think it is a good idea if the waste tax becomes more expensive for households with multiple people.

207326.explanation: We live in a time of housing shortage. It is important that both our housing seekers and status holders have an equal chance at housing. However, the municipality is obliged to house status holders. This can be done in a modest and efficient way through temporary housing.

207327.explanation: The current budget is sufficient. We also have not received signals that more budget is needed.

207329.explanation: Newcomers currently receive abundant offers for language lessons. There is no reason to assume this should change.

207331.explanation: We do not think this idea will work in practice. Schools must also want to cooperate. Instead, we want to help residents with limited means so every child has the opportunity to learn to swim!

207332.explanation: We have other priorities and choose an eastern ring road and a new swimming pool as the two major and expensive projects. Furthermore, we think a new large theater should be developed through an initiative taker.

207334.explanation: The Batenstein swimming pool in Woerden is old and dilapidated. It needs to be replaced by a new pool. The H2O swimming pool in Harmelen is a very different story. It is currently functioning excellently, and there is no reason to remove it.

207335.explanation: We opt for more deployment of community police officers instead of hiring extra enforcement officers (boa’s). We also deserve more community police officers than are currently on the streets.

207336.explanation: It depends on the situation. When justified, we can well imagine that more camera surveillance is needed. However, the privacy of passersby is also important.

207337.explanation: Good street lighting benefits safety. You must prevent unpleasant dark spots from forming. For this reason, the public space budget should be increased.

207338.explanation: In times of housing shortage, all efforts must be made to help starters buy a home more easily. A starter loan is an excellent instrument for this with lower interest than a bank.

207343.explanation: Extra attention for the preservation of the nature reserve 'de Kade', accessible from Haanwijk. We want to prevent the nature area from shrinking due to housing plans.

207344.explanation: We are strong advocates for demand-driven splitting of homes. The problem is often not the money but the surrounding regulations. We want to tackle those.

207345.explanation: LijstvanderDoes believes that no wind turbines should be built on land in our municipality. Not 3, not 6. But zero! Also not in neighboring municipalities. The health risks for humans and animals are too great, there is high uncertainty due to grid congestion, and insufficient support among the population.

207346.explanation: Assistance at a low-income threshold must be customized. This means each case is assessed individually for the need for help. Simply raising the income threshold is insufficient.

207347.explanation: If people are able to perform social work, we recognize its value. Positive developments for these people include having a fixed routine and staying in contact with society through daily conversations.

207348.explanation: LijstvanderDoes believes that the long-term unemployed should be helped, but not through subsidies. This can be done by contacts with entrepreneurs and organizations to help them get training and employment. Subsidies can also be cut away, leaving them back at square one.

207349.explanation: Residents with limited financial means have the greatest difficulty making ends meet when paying municipal taxes. We are already helping them, and we want to continue doing so.

207350.explanation: By law, the municipality is obliged to reimburse incomes up to the social assistance standard. Above that, we do not see it as a municipal responsibility.

207354.explanation: Lijst van der Does wants a 30 km/h speed limit in all residential neighborhoods and villages. However, main roads such as the Hollandbaan are designed for 50 km/h and that should remain so.

207355.explanation: It is not the best solution for every situation. There are examples where parking on the edge of the neighborhood works. But not always. For instance, when there is stacked construction, a parking garage on your own property is a better solution.

207356.explanation: For more than 30 years there has been a need for a bypass plus bridge on the west side of Woerden. Currently, traffic is gradually clogging up on this side due to through-traffic. Traffic that does not need to go through Woerden can then be diverted via the bypass.

207360.explanation: The first hour free was a great success and led to better use of parking garages. Also, in comparable cities like IJsselstein, the first 2 hours free have been a success for years and add extra appeal to the city center.

207362.explanation: We see the value in building heat networks as a voluntary alternative to gas. If there is sufficient support in the neighborhood, it is good if the municipality co-finances the realization of a heat network. Ideally, the municipality becomes the owner of the heat network.

207363.explanation: That task does not belong to the municipality, but to schools and other institutions.

207304.explanation: The VVD finds it important to involve residents in decisions. A referendum is not the right tool for this. It reduces complex problems to a simple 'yes' or 'no'. Our candidates know our city and three villages well. Therefore, we prefer to engage in dialogue with residents and entrepreneurs.

207306.explanation: The VVD is proud of residents who make plans to improve their neighborhoods. They can already receive subsidies for that. Money is not the problem; rather, initiatives still too often get stuck in bureaucracy or unnecessary rules. We want the municipality to be less of an obstacle and to think along better.

207307.explanation: The VVD finds it important not only to listen to those who shout the loudest or are asked to join a 'sounding board group.' We keep an eye on the silent majority. Lottery is not an ideal solution because many people with jobs are too busy to participate in such a group.

207310.explanation: The VVD is proud of the many (manufacturing) companies in our municipality. We want Woerden to remain attractive for these companies and their (future) employees. Therefore, we invest in accessible business parks, less bureaucracy, more vocational training and internships, and initiatives like the TechnoHUB.

207312.explanation: The VVD chooses more space for entrepreneurs. Therefore, we support more flexible opening hours, provided hospitality entrepreneurs are mindful of nearby residents and prevent nuisance. Companies can already submit all desired opening hours when applying for an operating license.

207314.explanation: The VVD wants to build many (owner-occupied) homes in the coming years. We want simpler procedures so that applications are processed faster. Making building permits cheaper also helps. Currently, a higher construction sum means higher costs, while the municipality does not automatically have more work.

207315.explanation: The VVD chooses more space for entrepreneurs. Therefore, we support more flexible opening hours, provided hospitality entrepreneurs are mindful of nearby residents and prevent nuisance. The mayor already sometimes allows terraces to stay open longer in summer. That goes well and can therefore happen more often.

207320.explanation: The VVD wants to speed up insulating houses because it lowers energy bills and reduces energy consumption. At this moment, your income does not matter for applying for an insulation subsidy. We keep it that way. Subsidies should also be available to residents with a middle income.

207321.explanation: The VVD chooses clean, green, and safe streets. Mowing less often can save municipal tax money and is good for biodiversity. However, preventing unsafe traffic situations is the VVD's top priority.

207322.explanation: The VVD chooses safe, clean, well-maintained, and green streets. Currently, there is still a lot of deferred maintenance. Therefore, we will get the basics in order. We use tax money efficiently. If a well-founded plan shows that more money is needed, we support that.

207323.explanation: The VVD wants residents to dispose of their waste as easily and cheaply as possible. We are against systems like diftar, where you pay per container. We encourage recycling in other ways. We will switch to post-separation as soon as it becomes more effective and cheaper than separate collection.

207324.explanation: The VVD chooses low and fair charges. Therefore, we want to charge waste collection fees per person instead of per household. This ensures that small households are no longer disadvantaged and costs are divided fairly. We keep these costs as low as possible anyway.

207326.explanation: The VVD finds it unfair to automatically give refugees who are allowed to stay priority for social rental housing just because they are 'status holders.' We look at individual situations to see if priority is needed. We provide temporary housing for status holders, such as flexible homes.

207327.explanation: The VVD believes newcomers should integrate and participate as quickly as possible. More money is not the best solution for this; immediately starting to learn Dutch and finding a job or volunteer work is. Therefore, we focus on better language lessons and cooperation with local employers.

207329.explanation: The VVD finds it crucial that newcomers learn Dutch as quickly as possible. Only then can they participate and work. We provide affordable courses to learn the language at the highest possible level. If you do not speak enough Dutch and consciously do not cooperate, we will reduce your benefits.

207331.explanation: The VVD wants parents to be able to choose the way and timing of their child’s swimming lessons. For schools, having all students swim through the school program is a big practical burden. The municipality already reimburses swimming lessons up to diploma C for children of low-income parents.

207332.explanation: The VVD chooses a cultural offer that grows with the number of inhabitants. We first want to investigate what is best: expanding Het Klooster along with renovating Wagenstraat, or a new larger theater. We are open to both options, provided they are financially realistic.

207334.explanation: Batenstein is very outdated and currently costs a lot for energy and maintenance. Renovation is only possible at enormous costs. Therefore, the VVD wants a centrally located, new and modern swimming pool. This will first replace Batenstein and eventually also (at least the 25-meter pool of) H2O.

207335.explanation: The VVD chooses safe streets. Community officers fight nuisance and violations and increase the visibility of enforcement. In the coming period, we want at least 2 extra community officers so that they can be present more often in the city, neighborhoods, and villages. We provide them with the right resources to do their work.

207336.explanation: The VVD chooses safe streets. Therefore, we will use camera surveillance more often in unsafe places or where there is nuisance. When cameras are installed, the chance of criminal and nuisance behavior is smaller. We will make it possible for residents to apply for (temporary) cameras with clear rules.

207337.explanation: The VVD wants you to always be able to walk safely on the streets. Therefore, we ensure good lighting of roads, cycling, and walking paths, also in rural areas. We never turn off lighting to save energy or protect nature if it makes it less safe.

207338.explanation: The VVD wants to build, build, build to help starters get affordable (owner-occupied) homes faster. Therefore, we increase the building pace, simplify procedures, and improve housing turnover. A targeted starter loan can give young people the extra push to buy a house in Woerden.

207343.explanation: The VVD wants people in Harmelen to be able to live affordably in their own village. Therefore, we must keep building, especially for starters and the elderly. The VVD believes that more houses fit in the Haanwijk polder than the 90 currently allowed by the province. We will make plans with the village for that.

207344.explanation: The VVD wants to encourage house splitting to create more affordable homes and promote turnover. Cadastral splitting is free, but structural splitting is sometimes unnecessarily expensive due to fees. It is even more important that the municipality thinks along and makes splitting homes easier.

207345.explanation: The municipal council has decided that multiple wind turbines may be installed in Reijerscop. The VVD opposed this and considers this the maximum for Woerden. Additional wind turbines are not an option for us. If the province would force Woerden to install more, we will clearly oppose that.

207346.explanation: The VVD believes that work should always pay off. Raising income limits generically makes poverty assistance unfocused and narrows the difference between working and not working. The VVD does not accept that. Working residents who struggle to make ends meet deserve targeted and temporary support.

207347.explanation: The VVD wants people on social assistance to get back to work or participate in society as soon as possible. Those who cannot (any longer) work can count on our support. However, we expect a counter-performance because we find it important that people keep participating.

207348.explanation: The VVD wants long-term unemployed people to find regular jobs as soon as possible. Subsidized jobs are not a permanent solution. The municipality must help them with guidance, retraining, and gaining work experience so people can sustainably return to paid work.

207349.explanation: The VVD believes that everyone should pay according to their means for the services we all use. Full exemption from municipal taxes leads to unfair situations and reduces the incentive to work. Therefore, we want to tighten the rules for exemptions.

207350.explanation: The VVD believes working should always pay off. A temporary supplement can help with the transition from social assistance to paid work. But it is better to earn your income yourself as quickly as possible. Permanent supplements discourage people from working more or advancing to a better job.

207354.explanation: The VVD prioritizes safety and flow in traffic. Traffic safety requires customized solutions. Therefore, we are against a general 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas. On main roads, traffic flow must remain good. We implement 30 km/h only where necessary, such as near schools or unsafe streets.

207355.explanation: The VVD wants you to easily and close to home park your car. Therefore, we ensure enough parking spaces in new neighborhoods. Rules about the number of parking spaces are meant to prevent parking nuisance, not to make Woerden residents give up their cars as a municipality decision.

207356.explanation: The VVD wants you to get from A to B quickly and safely. We do not want traffic that does not need to be in Woerden to drive through the city. The western bypass should remain possible in the future. We currently prioritize an eastern bypass because traffic congestion is increasing rapidly there.

207360.explanation: The VVD chooses to keep your expenses low and to have an attractive city center for residents and visitors. Therefore, we will examine if and how we can make parking in garages free for a few hours. In any case, we want parking in garages to be free between 10 p.m. and 10 a.m., just like on the street.

207362.explanation: The VVD chooses feasible alternatives to natural gas, with attention to every neighborhood. We are not against district heating networks if they are logical, profitable, remain affordable, and do not prevent better alternatives. Residents should be able to choose for themselves. We are critical of co-paying with public money.

207363.explanation: The VVD believes that people are responsible for a healthy lifestyle themselves. The municipality can support this with good information, as is already done in Woerden. This helps residents make more conscious choices without government paternalism. Therefore, we do not see the need for more effort.

207304.explanation: D66 supports the introduction of a citizens' assembly: a group of residents that forms a representative reflection of the municipality’s inhabitants. Additionally, we work on engaging residents in and with the neighborhoods and centers. By means of a children's mayor, we involve young people more in policy.

207306.explanation: Definitely! D66 Woerden believes that the residents of neighborhoods and centers know best what is needed to keep their neighborhood livable. Therefore, we allocate 20 euros per resident via the neighborhood and village platforms.

207307.explanation: D66 supports the introduction of a citizens' assembly: a group of residents that forms a representative reflection of the municipality’s inhabitants. For complex social issues, the citizens' assembly advises the municipal council.

207310.explanation: The municipality must ensure that Woerden is an attractive place for entrepreneurs, not just for a specific group of entrepreneurs. However, we do believe that businesses requiring a lot of space should find a place on the outskirts of the municipality.

207312.explanation: The municipality must establish (minimal) regulations in good consultation with hospitality operators and residents that consider all interests.

207314.explanation: We believe that municipal services should be cost-covering. Currently, the municipality is making a loss on issuing building permits.

207315.explanation: The municipality must establish (minimal) regulations in good consultation with hospitality operators and residents that consider all interests.

207320.explanation: Sustainability must also remain feasible for people with low incomes. It helps them with lower energy costs. Sustainability also contributes to a healthy life in Woerden—for our children and grandchildren as well. Ultimately, everyone benefits from investing in sustainability.

207321.explanation: Woerden already follows an advanced mowing policy that takes biodiversity promotion into account as much as possible. We support this policy.

207322.explanation: The current management of public space is modest and efficient, and we support that. Extra expenditures would come at the expense of other matters important to D66.

207323.explanation: Agreed. The municipality should continue working with a system where households pay less if they produce less residual waste.

207326.explanation: Politics must be honest about this. There is no alternative. Otherwise, the entire asylum chain gets clogged. Incidentally, only slightly more than 10% of social rental homes ultimately end up with recognized refugees.

207327.explanation: The municipality provides capacity to actively support organizations and initiatives that help asylum seekers and other newcomers in Woerden with their integration, learning the language, and finding work.

207329.explanation: Everything that contributes to the rapid integration of recognized refugees is good. Mastery of the Dutch language is perhaps the most important factor for recognized refugees to find a place in our society, including finding work and building a social network.

207331.explanation: It is important that all children in our watery municipality can swim. The municipality should provide for this as much as possible. Whether this should always be done through school swimming, in our view, is a question.

207332.explanation: A large theater must be established in Woerden. Of course, the municipality should not build it itself but should look for space and enable the development of a new theater.

207334.explanation: Ultimately, a large, new, modern, multifunctional swimming pool on the east side of Woerden is the best and most sustainable solution for our two outdated pools. We will ensure that the other functions, especially of H2O in Harmelen, can continue to exist. And H2O will remain open during construction.

207335.explanation: Woerden scores lower than average in terms of crime, and residents' sense of safety is better than average in comparable cities. Therefore, we believe we can better spend our money on other matters.

207336.explanation: The whole country, including Woerden, already has enough cameras in our view. Only if there is a very specific reason can cameras be installed in new locations.

207337.explanation: Basically, we believe there should be as little light pollution as possible at night. To increase social safety, however, good lighting must be installed in specific places.

207338.explanation: No. If people cannot get a loan at a bank, it is unwise for the municipality to take over the bank’s role.

207343.explanation: D66 Woerden has no taboos about solving the housing shortage by building houses. The Haanwijk polder is a location where, alongside green space, a new residential area can be developed.

207344.explanation: In solving the housing shortage by creating more homes, we believe there should be no taboos.

207345.explanation: We already contribute our share to the national target and would rather focus further on other forms of sustainable energy generation.

207346.explanation: The current arrangement is good. However, we believe exceptions should be possible. If it really cannot be done, people should be able to turn to the municipality for support.

207347.explanation: It is important to always encourage people to participate socially. That ultimately increases their chances of returning to work. But doing social work just because you might otherwise lose benefits goes too far for us.

207348.explanation: D66 believes everyone should be able to participate in society in a way that suits them. Sometimes that may be with a subsidized job. If there is a major shortage of subsidized jobs, more should be created. However, there are no signals that this is the case.

207349.explanation: It is important that everyone can make ends meet normally. There is significant poverty among working people with a low income. This waiver can then make the difference needed.

207350.explanation: As far as this occurs, it is a problem caused by national legislation. It is not feasible to solve this at the municipal level.

207354.explanation: Speed reduction increases livability and safety. This fits our vision of livable neighborhoods and villages, with priority for pedestrians and cyclists.

207355.explanation: This is good for livability and safety in the neighborhoods. It also offers opportunities for more greenery.

207356.explanation: So far, more traffic problems are solved with an eastern bypass.

207360.explanation: The parking garages are already not cost-covering. Making them even cheaper means losing budget for solving bigger problems.

207362.explanation: This is the responsibility of energy or infrastructure companies, not of municipalities.

207363.explanation: Good living is also healthy living. The municipality can provide facilities, but it is also important that residents know what they themselves can do, for themselves and for their children.

207304.explanation: Volt wants residents to think along about important choices. A referendum comes too late. The plan is then already fixed, and you can only say yes or no. Therefore, we engage with people earlier, for example through a citizens' assembly, so plans are made together and broadly supported.

207306.explanation: Volt wants to work with a participatory budget. This means that neighborhood residents decide for themselves about a part of the municipal money. We believe people know best what is good for their neighborhood. This way, good ideas get space and the neighborhood is improved together.

207307.explanation: Volt supports a citizens' assembly. This is a randomly selected group of residents that advises the municipality. We trust the knowledge and experience of residents. By involving them early and listening well, the municipality makes better choices.

207310.explanation: Woerden remains economically strong if there are companies offering practical jobs. The municipality must attract such companies and ensure clear rules and enough space in business parks. This way, people have work and the economy grows.

207312.explanation: Woerden has cozy cafés and restaurants where people like to go. This lively nightlife makes the city vibrant and attractive. To maintain and grow this, cafés and restaurants should be able to choose their own opening hours.

207314.explanation: Cheaper building permits make it easier to build. Building is important for everyone: this helps keep Woerden a livable and vital city for all.

207315.explanation: Woerden has cozy cafés and restaurants where people like to go. This lively nightlife makes the city vibrant and attractive. Terraces with generous opening hours are part of this.

207320.explanation: Volt believes that residents with a low income should receive help to better insulate their homes. This reduces their energy costs. It makes Woerden greener and helps prevent poverty.

207321.explanation: By mowing less often, nature gets more opportunities and the municipality saves money. Traffic safety must still be protected.

207322.explanation: More money is too vague to say yes or no to. It depends on what it is spent on and where the money comes from. Therefore, we first talk with residents in the neighborhood and on the street.

207323.explanation: Those who produce more residual waste pay more. This way, the polluter pays and people are encouraged to produce less waste. Together, we ensure a greener society.

207324.explanation: Volt sees that per resident payment is complicated. It can place extra burdens on families with children and increase poverty. It is better that people pay based on the actual amount of waste they throw away.

207326.explanation: Volt follows Dutch law, which states that refugees with a residence permit must receive housing. Woerden does no more than the national rules. This keeps contributions fair and the law is followed.

207327.explanation: Volt wants everyone to contribute to society. This keeps Woerden a pleasant place. Therefore, we help newcomers with language and culture and finding employment. This benefits everyone.

207329.explanation: Volt believes newcomers should take part in Woerden from day one. Language is necessary for this. Everyone has their own responsibility, but the municipality must also provide good lessons and facilities.

207331.explanation: Learning to swim is important for a child. Parents are responsible for this. Schools have other tasks and cannot provide swimming lessons. The municipality can financially help those in poverty and explain the importance of swimming lessons, so every child can learn to swim.

207332.explanation: Volt wants culture close by and accessible for everyone in all neighborhoods and villages. A large new theater is not a priority now. Projects by others are welcome and the municipality helps where needed, so culture can flourish for all.

207334.explanation: Renovating both swimming pools is expensive and causes long closures. A new swimming pool can provide enough space for everyone. This allows swimming lessons to continue and sports to remain possible. Later, H2O can receive a different sports function so it remains the pleasant place it is now.

207335.explanation: Volt wants to deploy the current special enforcement officers better: less on parking, more on safety. The money for extra officers can be better spent on measures that truly improve the feeling of safety.

207336.explanation: More cameras can help safety, but only in busy places such as near the station or shops. Privacy is important, and the cameras are managed only by European companies.

207337.explanation: Everyone must feel safe in the rural area. More streetlights help with this and make it more pleasant to be outside during the day and evening.

207338.explanation: A starter’s loan does not really help and can make houses more expensive. The municipality is not a bank. The real problem is the shortage of houses. Volt wants more affordable homes to make starting easier.

207343.explanation: More houses are needed. In Haanwijk, more can be built than currently planned. However, mainly affordable homes, not only expensive houses. This helps more people find a place to live.

207344.explanation: Splitting homes must be made easier. Currently, it is too complicated. Simplifying rules can create more homes and help address the housing shortage.

207345.explanation: With three wind turbines, Woerden meets the agreements with the province. An agreement is an agreement. For future plans, the municipality must better listen to residents’ concerns about nuisance, safety, and nature and take these seriously.

207346.explanation: Raising the income limit does not address the cause of poverty. Moreover, it harms those who need help the most. Volt wants policies that solve problems structurally instead of applying temporary fixes.

207347.explanation: Participation is good, but people should not be forced. What someone does must help them find work later. It is about learning and growing, progressing and contributing, not about having to do something.

207348.explanation: Subsidized jobs often help only temporarily. Volt wants real jobs in a strong local economy so people can work permanently.

207349.explanation: Remission helps low-income people simply and effectively. It increases equal opportunities and prevents bigger problems. This instrument works and must remain.

207350.explanation: Temporary supplements can make the step to work easier. It prevents working from initially costing money. This must be tailored and temporary, so working always pays off.

207354.explanation: Experiences from other cities show that 30 km/h zones increase safety, reduce noise, and improve livability. Volt wants to introduce this, except on major through roads.

207355.explanation: Volt wants new neighborhoods with good livability. There will be enough greenery and safe roads for cyclists and pedestrians. Completely banning cars is not realistic, but parking will have a place without the neighborhood being negatively affected.

207356.explanation: There is already a Western ring road, namely the Burgemeester Van Zwietenweg. That connection works well. For other roads, it must first be clear what problem a new road will solve.

207360.explanation: Free parking can make the center more attractive. Volt wants to consider this as support for local entrepreneurs, alongside promoting cycling.

207362.explanation: District heating networks are complex and expensive. A clear cost-benefit analysis is needed. Volt sees them as an option for new neighborhoods, but not as a first general municipal investment.

207363.explanation: Volt wants healthcare providers to have more time for prevention. This way, illness and absenteeism are prevented and healthcare costs stay lower. Residents receive more attention and remain healthier, which is good for everyone and the municipality.

207304.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP finds it important that residents participate early via clear participation processes and the right to challenge. A referendum is less fitting with our focus on dialogue with clear frameworks in advance and reliable execution by elected council members.

207306.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to provide space and support for initiatives but first focuses on reducing administrative burden and utilizing existing resources. Extra money is not needed as long as current budgets are sufficient and not fully utilized.

207307.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to improve participation through clear processes and existing advisory councils. A randomly selected group requires much support and structural costs. We choose well-facilitated participation with clear agreements beforehand.

207310.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to strengthen the economy via education, entrepreneurs, government, and good locations. Not with 'locational bonuses'. This way, we sustainably build the manufacturing industry.

207312.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP chooses clarity and balance between liveliness and livability. Just as for shopkeepers, opening hours also apply for cafés and restaurants. Room for customization remains possible through the General Local Ordinance (APV).

207314.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP finds it important that the price is cost-covering. More important to them is that granting building permits proceeds more smoothly and simply.

207315.explanation: Expansion can already be done with the mayor's permission. The ChristenUnie-SGP is not in favor of structural expansion; customization where necessary.

207320.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in the energy transition. The ChristenUnie-SGP focuses on insulation assistance through the Sustainability Shop and insulation teams and establishes schemes for low-income households.

207321.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to keep verges and grass fields safe and tidy. We mow less where possible to promote biodiversity; where visibility or safety requires it, we mow more frequently.

207322.explanation: Over the past years and in the coming years, the budget for managing public spaces is increasing. This gradually improves the quality of public space. We choose investments that strengthen livability the most, within a realistic financial framework.

207323.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to encourage waste separation without side effects. Higher costs for residual waste increase the risk of illegal dumping and contamination of other waste streams. Extra charges can be applied above a reasonable threshold; not under normal use.

207324.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to keep the system simple and predictable. We focus first on better waste separation. Higher costs for residual waste increase the risk of illegal dumping and contamination of other waste streams. This is not beneficial to the environment.

207326.explanation: We have an obligation to house these residents. Since there are no alternative homes, social rental housing is appropriate. The ChristenUnie-SGP recognizes that status holders are prioritized over residents who have been waiting longer. To reduce waiting times, more construction is needed.

207327.explanation: After integration, ongoing support and language education are necessary to find work and participate in society. This requires commitment and resources.

207329.explanation: Language is essential for integration. The ChristenUnie-SGP wants new residents to continue taking language lessons even after their integration process. This way, new residents become self-reliant faster, can participate in society sooner, and find work more quickly.

207331.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants safe and affordable swimming education for every child through targeted support/schemes, without the heavy organizational burden (for schools) of school swimming.

207332.explanation: Our current theater is unique and the ChristenUnie-SGP wants to preserve that. Neighboring cities have larger theaters that are easily accessible by public transport. A theater is not a profit model and always means higher costs for the community.

207334.explanation: Two swimming pools are no longer financially feasible. The ChristenUnie-SGP wants a new swimming complex in a central location. Batenstein and H2O should remain open as long as possible. The current H2O location should retain space for community gatherings.

207335.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) are important for ensuring our safety. Four BOAs in Woerden is below average. Therefore, the ChristenUnie-SGP wants more BOAs.

207336.explanation: Camera surveillance is a means, not an end. In the interest of safety, it must be possible to deploy camera surveillance.

207337.explanation: Where safety is unmistakably at risk, we will install extra lighting. The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to prevent unnecessary light pollution and energy costs.

207338.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP focuses on additional construction and facilitating housing flow. Starter loans do not solve the housing shortage and can drive up prices.

207343.explanation: The 90 planned homes are an asset for Harmelen. Deciding now that no more homes can be built is premature.

207344.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to facilitate splitting homes where it can be done with good quality. Regulatory pressure should be reduced to add suitable homes.

207345.explanation: The starting point is to meet the local energy generation target, integrating it in Reijerscop with connections to neighboring municipalities. The absolute number of wind turbines is not the point, but the amount of energy generated is what matters.

207346.explanation: There is a group of (working) residents living in poverty due to high fixed costs. This group currently receives no assistance. The ChristenUnie-SGP finds it important that the municipality also pays attention to these residents.

207347.explanation: Every resident should be encouraged to do socially meaningful work. This benefits both the resident and their environment. We cannot force residents. Some residents are unable to perform social work. Their social benefits should not depend on this.

207348.explanation: Using wage cost subsidies and guaranteed jobs helps residents re-enter the labor market, and not all costs are borne by the employer. Participating in the labor market provides satisfaction, income, and a network.

207349.explanation: Remission of debts can help to prevent (further) accumulation of debt.

207350.explanation: The social benefit is based on the minimum wage. For a short period, this can help someone find a place in the labor market again.

207354.explanation: When redesigning streets and/or neighborhoods, 30 km/h is the standard for street and residential access layout.

207355.explanation: Parking spaces at the edges of the neighborhood create more space for greenery and play areas in the immediate living environment.

207356.explanation: If more housing is chosen in Woerden-West, that must be combined with a plan for a western bypass road.

207360.explanation: The first two hours of free parking mean a sharp increase in the hourly rate. The ChristenUnie-SGP prefers to maintain a low hourly rate.

207362.explanation: The installation of district heating networks is funded by national government resources. The ChristenUnie-SGP believes this should remain so.

207363.explanation: The municipality and the Public Health Service (GGD) already engage in health prevention. Residents themselves must also take responsibility.

207304.explanation: Not all topics are suitable for a referendum. But some single-issue questions are, and we support that. Progressief Woerden mainly favors other forms of better involving citizens in decision-making, such as through a citizens' council.

207306.explanation: We encourage citizen initiatives and support neighborhood and village platforms. We ensure residents receive assistance to realize their plans. This is the work of neighborhood connectors — support workers whom you can approach with questions and ideas about the neighborhood.

207307.explanation: To involve as many residents as possible in decision-making, we want to experiment with new forms of participation, both online and offline. This means you can have a say about plans in your neighborhood. We also want to involve young people.

207310.explanation: We want the municipality to actively encourage companies to offer internships and jobs for all young people, regardless of their background. Of course, the municipality should set a good example itself.

207312.explanation: We want a vibrant nightlife in Woerden, but without nuisance. We have BOAs for dealing with nuisance, and a fixed closing time does not help with that. In fact, a fixed closing time causes everyone to leave at the same time, which increases nuisance.

207314.explanation: Processing a building permit costs money because municipal staff work on it. As far as we are concerned, the applicant should pay those costs — no more, no less.

207315.explanation: Woerden may be lively, but we must prevent nuisance. Progressief Woerden supports free closing times for the hospitality industry. Terrace opening hours are fine as they are.

207320.explanation: We continue the successful subsidies for making homes more sustainable. In addition, we will specifically help homeowners' associations. Together with Cazas, we will further improve the sustainability of social rental homes.

207321.explanation: In places where this does not lead to unsafe traffic situations, the municipality should let nature take its course. Not everywhere short-mown grass, but flowering plants instead.

207322.explanation: The management of public space must be safe and cost-conscious. It does not have to be luxurious, but should be good enough.

207323.explanation: Woerden has sufficient facilities to separate almost all household waste. Processing residual waste costs the municipality a lot of money, and those who do not sufficiently separate their waste should pay for that.

207324.explanation: According to Progressief Woerden, the number of residents per household is not the most important factor. Waste levies should be as much as possible dependent on how much residual waste a household produces, because that costs the municipality money.

207326.explanation: In our opinion, everyone in an urgent situation should be given priority for a social rental home. This also includes refugees with residence permits, but not only them. The most important thing is to build many more affordable homes.

207327.explanation: Good integration and speaking the language are important for successful participation in Woerden society. This benefits the newcomer themselves, but also the rest of Woerden.

207329.explanation: We encourage initiatives that help refugees and other newcomers quickly get started with work or education. This is good for the refugee themselves, but also for the rest of Woerden.

207331.explanation: We find it important that all children in Woerden can swim and therefore want to reintroduce school swimming lessons for all children in group 4 or 5. This also makes life much easier for working parents.

207332.explanation: We believe it is important that cultural and sports facilities grow along with the increasing population. Currently, almost all performances are sold out. We will explore possibilities for a new (larger) theatre in the center or in the station area.

207334.explanation: If a new swimming pool is built somewhere between Woerden and Harmelen, then it is negotiable for both existing swimming pools to close.

207335.explanation: Preventing street nuisance requires more than just enforcement. We must ensure that youth can entertain themselves in places where they do not cause nuisance. Remaining nuisance will be tackled by current enforcement officers, while ensuring there is no discrimination.

207336.explanation: Cameras give a false sense of security. It is better to design public spaces clearly and help residents properly protect their belongings.

207337.explanation: On important cycling routes, residents must be able to travel safely, and good lighting is part of that. On other routes, we are cautious because nature benefits from less light.

207338.explanation: People have the right to an affordable home. The most important thing is to build enough affordable homes. If a starter loan is the best solution, we support that. But other instruments to make affordable housing available are also open for discussion as far as we are concerned.

207343.explanation: By building specifically in Haanwijk in Harmelen, Kamerik Northeast, and near the renewed Milandhof (together with the new Jorai School) in Zegveld, more opportunities arise for both youth and elderly. This helps schools and shops (and other facilities) remain open.

207344.explanation: We do not only build traditionally. Flex homes, tiny houses, and splitting larger homes also create extra living space.

207345.explanation: With these three wind turbines, Woerden meets the agreements we made in the Climate Accord. That is sufficient for now.

207346.explanation: Not only your income determines whether you make ends meet, but also your expenses. Prices have risen sharply in recent years. As a result, the income threshold may need to be increased.

207347.explanation: It is good that people receiving benefits are encouraged to participate as much as possible in society, for example through (volunteer) work. Coercion is ineffective.

207348.explanation: We appreciate socially engaged entrepreneurs in Woerden, many of whom do their best to employ people with distance to the labor market. Progressief Woerden believes the municipality should continually review what entrepreneurs need to make this possible.

207349.explanation: Poverty is only growing in the Netherlands. For this target group, exemption from charges is important to keep afloat.

207350.explanation: It should not be possible that someone loses income by continuing to work.

207354.explanation: Perhaps not on the main roads. But definitely in all residential neighborhoods. That will only make the city safer.

207355.explanation: In the new neighborhoods near the station, we favor lower parking standards. But the cars that are there should preferably be parked underground in parking garages, not on the edge of the neighborhood.

207356.explanation: A western ring road hardly solves any problems and is unaffordable. In the east of Woerden (Oudelandseweg, Utrechtsestraatweg, and Steinhagenseweg) the need for better accessibility is greater.

207360.explanation: Free parking does not exist. It costs the municipality tens of thousands of euros per month. This must be paid for by all residents.

207362.explanation: In Molenvliet, the cheapest, most sustainable, and most attractive method of heating homes is being researched. If the conclusion is that a heating network benefits our residents, the municipality should contribute financially.

207363.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. The municipality has a role in helping people make healthy choices. This can be done through campaigns, but also through welfare work or sports and exercise coaches.

207304.explanation: The CDA wants to involve residents well in municipal decision-making through participation. We do not consider referendums a good tool for involving residents in municipal decisions. Difficult issues are often oversimplified to a yes or no in referendums.

207306.explanation: The CDA wants to work on improving all villages and neighborhoods in the municipality. For this, we want the municipality to collaborate well with neighborhood and village platforms and engage with nearby residents. But allocating money to a group of residents for this is not a process we favor.

207307.explanation: The CDA is not against citizen assemblies that advise the municipality. It is one of many tools to involve residents. Citizen assemblies can help build trust between residents and politics and can provide valuable input for municipal policy.

207310.explanation: The CDA wants to ensure Woerden is attractive for all types of businesses. We do this, among other things, by providing good business parks. It is not necessary to allocate funds specifically to attract a single business type. There should be business space and work opportunities for everyone.

207312.explanation: The CDA favors a local business climate with less regulatory pressure, but there are valid agreements about hospitality opening hours. We do not want a proliferation of opening times that lead to reduced livability, peace, and social cohesion, especially in the city center.

207314.explanation: The CDA considers it important that building permit costs are not an obstacle to housing construction. Construction in the municipality should be encouraged so that enough homes are built. Cheaper building permits can help with this.

207315.explanation: The CDA wishes everyone to enjoy a pleasant evening on the terrace. That is why the municipality of Woerden has generous opening hours (until midnight on weekdays and until 1 AM on weekends). We also consider the livability and peace for local residents and therefore do not want later opening hours.

207320.explanation: The CDA supports maintaining the insulation fund. This fund helps residents insulate their homes. This way even people with lower incomes can insulate their homes. An insulated house offers more living comfort and results in a lower energy bill.

207321.explanation: The CDA finds the current municipal mowing policy good. There is already less mowing than before to stimulate biodiversity. Mowing even less is not good for the maintenance of our public spaces. Therefore, the CDA is not in favor of mowing less.

207322.explanation: The CDA wants more funds to become available for public spaces in the long term. We want good plans for managing public spaces in a modest and efficient manner. To keep public spaces safe, clean, and beautiful, we will need to spend more on management.

207323.explanation: The CDA wants people to be encouraged to separate waste properly. Therefore, it is good that residual waste carries a small fee. This encourages proper sorting of paper, organic waste, and plastic. Those who put out their gray bin less often are rewarded for their behavior.

207324.explanation: The CDA sees no need to change the waste levy. A system charging per person would have to differentiate by age to include children. This brings a lot of administrative burdens to solve a problem that does not exist.

207326.explanation: The CDA wants status holders in our municipality to integrate quickly. Finding housing is important for this. Only a small portion of homes go to status holders. Removing their priority does not help Woerden housing seekers much and hampers refugees in participating in society.

207327.explanation: The CDA finds it important that newcomers integrate well. However, not everything is a matter of money. Good contact between Ferm Werk and companies helps newcomers find work. There are more initiatives. We are working on a climate that promotes integration.

207329.explanation: The CDA wants newcomers to learn Dutch well. However, more is needed than language lessons alone. Participating in society also helps with language learning. Not everything needs to be learned in the classroom. A proper level of language lessons for the basics remains necessary.

207331.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of school swimming lessons. Schools already have many responsibilities. Providing swimming lessons during school hours is not one of them for us. Parents should have the freedom to choose where and when their child takes swimming lessons.

207332.explanation: The CDA believes the size of Klooster is right for Woerden. It is used as a stage for local artists and serves as a test location for bigger names. A larger theater could cause these functions to disappear. A large theater would also not be financially viable. Gouda and Utrecht are nearby.

207334.explanation: The CDA supports building a new swimming pool in the municipality. Batenstein has reached the end of its lifespan and a completely new pool would be a good replacement. H2O is newer and can still be used for some time. For now, H2O does not need to close, in our opinion.

207335.explanation: The CDA supports adding at least 2 more municipal enforcement officers (boa's). This way, municipal regulations can be properly enforced and nuisance in neighborhoods can be tackled. Safety in the municipality improves when more enforcers keep an eye on things.

207336.explanation: The CDA wants the safest possible municipality. In places where we know unsafe situations occur or shady activities take place, it is important to introduce camera surveillance.

207337.explanation: The CDA wants traffic safety and social safety in the rural areas to be well maintained. Lighting is important for this. Currently, lighting in the rural area is generally sufficient. In specific unsafe situations, extra lighting can be a suitable solution.

207338.explanation: The CDA wants to help first-time buyers by building as many houses as possible in the coming period. Starter loans are not a good way to help first-time buyers. They drive up prices for starter homes and benefit only the few lucky ones who manage to buy a house.

207343.explanation: The CDA sees opportunities at Haanwijk to realize much-needed homes for residents of Harmelen. Housing in this area must fit well with the surroundings and provide a pleasant living environment. In our view, more than 90 homes can be built under these conditions.

207344.explanation: The CDA sees splitting homes as one possible opportunity to create more housing supply. However, splitting homes is not always a suitable solution and therefore does not have to be automatically cheaper.

207345.explanation: The CDA wants to focus, for now, on the realization of wind turbines at Reijerscop. Wind turbines in other locations in the municipality are, in our opinion, not currently an option.

207346.explanation: The CDA wants to combat poverty in the municipality of Woerden. Raising the income threshold is, in our view, not a good measure for this. For low-income workers, dependency on municipal support can mean falling into a poverty trap if their income from work increases.

207347.explanation: The CDA finds it important that everyone participates in society. People receiving social assistance benefits must continue to search for paid work but can — as long as they don’t have paid work yet — do volunteer work or other socially relevant activities.

207348.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage entrepreneurs to hire people who face barriers to the labor market. The municipality should remove obstructive rules for this. However, it is not the municipality's task to actively create these jobs itself.

207349.explanation: The CDA wants to prevent people with very limited means from getting into trouble due to municipal taxes. Forgiveness of taxes must remain possible for those who truly cannot pay.

207350.explanation: The CDA wants to prevent the poverty trap, where people become financially worse off by losing benefits when they work more. By not immediately stopping the benefit completely beyond a certain threshold, this situation is prevented. This way, working more always pays off.

207354.explanation: The CDA supports lowering the maximum speed within built-up areas to 30 km/h in places where it is appropriate and improves safety. However, there are also plenty of roads that are safe and good at the current 50 km/h. So speed should not be lowered everywhere.

207355.explanation: The CDA finds it undesirable if people can only park their car at the edge of the neighborhood. Our starting point is that people park as much as possible on their own property in new construction projects. Apartment complexes should therefore have their own parking facilities.

207356.explanation: The CDA currently sees no realistic possibilities for building a western ring road. Now that a new bridge over the Oude Rijn will not be built, this is no longer a logical option. However, we are looking for other solutions to traffic bottlenecks in Woerden-West.

207360.explanation: The CDA is satisfied with the current tariff system in the parking garage. The first hour of parking is free. We want to keep it that way. Making the second hour free as well is, in our view, not necessary.

207362.explanation: The CDA wants a future-proof heating system when natural gas is phased out. The installation of heat networks can help with this. We prefer that the municipality controls this rather than a private party. The costs of a heat network could then be borne by the municipality.

207363.explanation: The CDA would like the municipality to promote healthy living. This includes providing information about healthy living, for example about nutrition and exercise, but also about social initiatives, because combating loneliness is also good for health.

statements.title: The terraces in Woerden must be allowed to stay open longer.
Low-income residents of Woerden must continue to receive municipal funds to insulate their homes.
The municipality must provide more funding for residents to improve their neighborhood.
The municipality must invest in attracting companies that employ many vocationally trained workers.
The municipality must mow roadside verges and grass fields less often.
The municipality should provide newcomers with more language classes.

titleParts.glossary: People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Households should pay for the amount of residual waste they produce; more residual waste therefore means higher costs.

titleParts.text: Huishoudens moeten betalen voor de hoeveelheid

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.
Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.

statements.title: The municipality should not build more houses in the Haanwijk polder near Harmelen than the 90 homes already planned.
The municipality should top up the income of residents who accept a job after being on welfare to the amount they received before.
Woerden should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety. There are currently four.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden. Nu zijn er 4.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance. More people would then receive help.
The municipality must co-fund the construction of district heating networks.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meebetalen aan de aanleg van

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.title: Woerden should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: There must be many more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.title: The residents of Woerden should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Woerden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Woerden should install more streetlights in the outlying/rural areas.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Woerden should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: Woerden moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should stop fully waiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het volledig

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Woerden should provide residents with more information about healthy living.
The municipality should build a new swimming pool in Woerden. The Batenstein and H2O pools would then close.
There should be a bypass along the west side of the municipality.
The municipality should build a new, larger theater.
Woerden should make the first two hours of parking in a downtown parking garage free.
In new neighborhoods, parking spaces should be located at the edge of the neighborhood.
Splitting a house into separate units should be made cheaper in Woerden.
A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
Municipal building permits should be cheaper.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
The municipality should allocate more funds for the management of public space.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Woerden should calculate the waste disposal charge per resident instead of per household. At present you pay either for one person or for two persons or more.
No more wind turbines may be built in Woerden than the three in the Reijerscop polder that have already been approved.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
